# AFL Team Rename / Rebrand

Learnings for renaming an AFL franchise and swapping its artwork (e.g. a loser-punishment name reverting back).

## 2026-06-27 - Where an AFL team name+artwork actually lives

**Context:** Reverting franchise `0023` from "Cock Gobbler" back to "The Show", including icon/banner artwork.

**Insight:** A franchise's displayed identity is spread across **three** hand-maintained files, plus two auto-generated traps:

1. **`data/afl-fantasy/afl.config.json`** — the **live, canonical** team config. `chooseTeamName()` and every AFL page import this. Update `name`, `nameMedium`, `nameShort`, `abbrev`, `aliases`, `icon`, `banner` on the matching `franchiseId` entry. Put the *old* name into `aliases` so search/lookups still resolve it.
2. **`data/afl-fantasy/afl.assets.json`** — feeds the assets gallery + `team-preferences.ts` (which looks up by `id`, so its `name` field is cosmetic). It is auto-generated by `scripts/sync-afl-assets.mjs`, **but do NOT regenerate to rename** — the generator pulls team `name`/`aliases` from the legacy `config.json` (see trap below), so a regen reintroduces stale names. Hand-edit the `name` and add the new asset entries to the `icons`/`group-me` arrays instead.
3. **`src/data/afl-constitution.ts`** — the `DIVISION SETUP` block lists every team as `Name (id)` and is fed to the **AFL rules chatbot** (`src/pages/api/afl-rules-qa.ts`). Rename here too or Roger keeps using the old name.

**Traps:**
- **`data/afl-fantasy/config.json` is dead** — no imports anywhere (`grep -rln afl-fantasy/config src`), and it still holds pre-historic names (e.g. `0023` = "Bring the Pain"). Ignore it; don't bother updating it. It's also why the assets-sync generator produces stale names.
- **Auto-synced files keep the old name and that's correct** — `mfl-feeds/**`, `standings.json`, `schefter-feed.json`, `awards-history.json` are historical snapshots from MFL cron syncs. Leave them; they reflect what was true at the time and update on the next sync once MFL itself is renamed.
- **This repo is display-only.** Renaming here does not rename the franchise on MyFantasyLeague. If the official MFL name must change, that's a separate manual step on MFL.

**Evidence:** `data/afl-fantasy/afl.config.json` (franchise `0023`), `scripts/sync-afl-assets.mjs` lines ~60-98 (reads `team.name`/`team.aliases`), `src/data/afl-constitution.ts:38`.

**Recommendation:** For any AFL rename, edit exactly those three files. Validate JSON (`node -e "JSON.parse(...)"`) and confirm resolution with a quick `cfg.teams.find(f=>f.franchiseId===...)` script.

## 2026-06-27 - Team artwork URLs are absolute production CDN paths

**Context:** Generated new `the_show.png` icon/group-me locally; they didn't appear in the local dev preview.

**Insight:** `afl.config.json` `icon`/`banner` are **absolute** `https://mflfootballv2.vercel.app/assets/afl/...` URLs (the repo pattern for all teams), not site-relative paths. So a local dev server loads team art from **production**, not from your working tree. New/changed art only renders visually after the branch deploys and the files ship to the CDN. The **text rename** is fully testable locally; the **art** is not.

**Asset specs** (match existing files): icon `100×100`, group-me `400×400`, banner `950×158`. For a quick placeholder, letterbox the banner's wordmark onto a square canvas with PIL/sharp, then apply a supersampled circular alpha mask if a circular crop is wanted. Verify with `file <png>` (dimensions + `RGBA` for transparency).

**Recommendation:** When verifying a rename locally, assert on the DOM text (`<span>The Show</span>`) rather than expecting the new icon to render. Tell the user the art lands on deploy.
